name: Ellafi
description_short: Connecticut-based federal credit union dedicated to empowering women and allies with personalized banking, loans, mortgages, and financial education resources.
description_long:
Ellafi Federal Credit Union is a Connecticut-based, member-owned credit union that launched approximately one year before April 2026 (around April 2025) with a mission to empower women, their families, and supportive allies. The credit union operates locations in Middletown and Meriden, Connecticut, and is federally insured through the NCUA. The organization traces its heritage to a predecessor institution known as Seasons, which served the community for decades before rebranding as Ellafi to reflect its evolved mission.

Ellafi offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, share certificates, credit cards, personal loans, microloans (up to $400), auto loans, home improvement loans, energy loans, mortgages with preapproval and closing support, and business solutions for entrepreneurs. The credit union also provides seasonal promotional products like the Amplifi Summer Loan (rates as low as 6.74% APR). Members can access coin deposit services at 10,000+ participating Coinstar kiosks nationwide, and the credit union emphasizes fraud prevention and account security.

Ellafi distinguishes itself through explicit gender-equity focus, positioning itself as Connecticut's first credit union dedicated to empowering women and non-binary individuals. The organization provides personalized financial recommendations, financial wellness programs, accessible financial education, and community support including scholarship opportunities (three $1,000 Ellafi Kind Scholarships for high school seniors). The company emphasizes demystifying personal finance and positioning members as capable of financial independence rather than "bad with money."

As a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union, Ellafi operates under standard credit union regulatory frameworks and provides NCUA insurance on deposits. The institution's specific focus on women and allies is genuine but may limit appeal for those seeking gender-neutral banking. Like all credit unions, membership eligibility may have requirements, though these are not detailed on the website. The organization appears operationally sound with recent awards (2026 Diamond Award for Best Credit Union Logo Design) and active community engagement.
services: Mortgages with preapproval and closing support
Coin deposit via Coinstar kiosks (10,000+ locations)
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure with NCUA insurance on deposits
Explicit focus on underserved populations with personalized financial tools and education programs
Microloans up to $400 for emergency gaps without lengthy approval processes
Mortgage services with clear preapproval and closing support tailored to applicants
Business solutions including payroll management, payment acceptance, and funding access
Coin deposit service at 10,000+ Coinstar kiosks for convenient cash deposits
Community investment including $1,000 scholarships for graduating high school seniors
Seasonal loan products (Amplifi Summer Loan) with competitive rates (6.74% APR)
cons: Limited to two physical locations (Middletown and Meriden, Connecticut) restricts geographic access
Membership eligibility requirements not transparent on website; may exclude some applicants
Gender-focused mission may not appeal to or serve traditional banking customers equally
No mention of online banking capabilities or mobile app features despite being 2026
Limited information on loan terms, APR ranges, or approval criteria for major products
best_for: Women seeking banking services from an institution explicitly committed to gender equity and financial independence
Connecticut residents needing personalized financial education and wellness support alongside banking services
Small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking supportive lending relationships
Community members prioritizing member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over corporate banks
diagnosis: Ellafi is best for Connecticut residents—particularly women and supportive allies—seeking values-aligned banking with a credit union that prioritizes financial education and personal empowerment. The main caveat is geographic limitation (Middletown/Meriden locations only) and unclear membership eligibility requirements, which may restrict access for out-of-state or ineligible applicants.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Ellafi — Credit Union in Middletown, CT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Ellafi — NCUA-insured credit union in Middletown, CT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
